rulings.Case Title: JERRY WAYNE KING VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 13, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Honeywell International, Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s claim for punitive damages: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45am. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY WILLIAMS, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR et al VS. DOES 1 THROUGH 75, INCLUSIVE
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 13, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant B.E. Development Corporation¿s demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs the opportunity, if they are able to do so, to cure the statute of limitations bar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RONNIE CAMERON VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 13, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Eaton Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD KEELING VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 13, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Pneumo Abex LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his asbestos-related illness. Defendant¿s moving papers made clear that one of the grounds upon which it sought summary judgment was that plaintiff has no evidence that exposure to asbestos-containing Abex products was a substantial factor in his illness. Plaintiff was on notice that defendant sought summary judgment on this ground, had an opportunity to respond, and failed to do so. (See undisputed material fact no. 37. See also Luri v. Greenwald (2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 1119, 1125; Juge v. County of Sacramento (1993) 12 Cal.App.4th 59.) The court will note that defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess evidence that he was exposed to a product attributable to defendant. Even if Mr. Indelicato¿s declaration is considered, when read in conjunction with plaintiff¿s deposition testimony it merely creates a triable issue whether the brake linings at issue were manufactured, sold and/or supplied by Abex. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MARGARET SIEFKEN, AS WRONGFUL DEATH HEIR, AND AS et al VS. KELLY-MOORE PAINT COMPANY, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 13, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc.¿s general demurrer to plaintiffs¿ complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. Plaintiffs did not substantively oppose defendant¿s demurrer. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
